investigation_results.root_cause: retreat bonus (+80) cannot overcome turn_rule_engine -1000 penalty when attack is available

experiment_1200_bonus.description: Increased F0003 retreat bonus from +80 to +1200 to overcome -1000
experiment_1200_bonus.decision: reject - retreat-based approach at this magnitude causes safety regressions

analysis.why_threshold_change_wont_help: All remaining 31 cases have Voltorb damage >= 240, so lowering threshold to 230 would catch 0 additional cases
analysis.why_bonus_increase_is_dangerous: Boosting retreat bonus to overcome turn_rule -1000 causes Bellibolt to retreat at inappropriate times, creating retreat_when_attack_available regressions
analysis.fundamental_limitation: turn_rule_engine universally penalizes retreat by -1000 when attack is available. This is correct 99% of the time but blocks pivot-to-better-attacker. Solving this requires architectural change, not parameter tuning.
alternative_approaches: Add a 'pivot_to_better_attacker' exception in turn_rule_engine that conditionally reduces the -1000 penalty
Add a Bellibolt attack penalty in ionos_rules when Voltorb has higher damage, instead of boosting retreat
Implement active Pokemon selection logic that prefers Voltorb during TO_ACTIVE/SWITCH contexts
Accept current level as sufficient (F0003 already reduced 57% from original 86)

recommendation: Maintain threshold=240 with bonus=+80. Further improvement requires turn_rule_engine architecture change or Bellibolt attack penalty approach, not threshold tuning.
